creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Daniel Engberg from comment #0)
> Given the size of the project instead of patching Makefile, pulling in
> gmake and pkgconfig just add small do-build section instead.
I honestly don't see what's the win here.  Doing it with explicit `do-build' target is understandable initial reaction seeing only one *.c file, and often could be as good idea, but IMHO not in this case:

1. Hard-coding pkg-config(8) output is ugly and error-prone: next update to `devel/glib20' port might change include paths or required libraries, and recipe would break.  This approach defeats the whole idea behind pkg-config(8), it's a step in the wrong direction;

2. One might argue that we could still use `do-build' together with calling pkg-config(8) somewhere in the port's Makefile (wrapped in a variable).  This is bad because it's syntactically heavy, requires special care WRT expansion time, and unnecessarily exposes internal build details on the port's level, we typically try to avoid that;

3. It's plain ugly.  Currently, program's Makefile requires just one simple gcc -> ${CC} fix, which you propose to replace with imperative cryptic commands.  This goes against declarative nature of port's Makefile we generally adhere to;

4. GNU make and pkg-config(8) are so tiny and lightweight that hardly anyone would notice them; in fact, they are so common that likely are already installed on the user's system.

> While at it, define LICENSE_FILE and do some minor changes in port
> Makefile to follow layout closer to the tree in general.
Suggested MASTER_SITES/WWW changes are gratuitous, violate DRY principle, PHB recommendation, and cause needless redirection (see below; we prefer URLs which return 200 OK).  LICENSE_FILE is not needed because provided file is just a standard GPL boilerplate without actual author/copyright information.

  $ curl -I http://www.leonerd.org.uk/code/xseticon
  HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently
  Location: /code/xseticon/
